title: Halogenoalkanes
summary: 9701 AS halogenoalkanes — SN1 and SN2 mechanisms, elimination, and environmental issues (CFCs/HCFCs).
learningObjectives: Classify halogenoalkanes as primary, secondary, or tertiary.
Compare SN1 and SN2 mechanisms and conditions favouring each.
Describe elimination reactions competing with substitution.
Discuss ozone depletion linked to halogenoalkanes.

simpleExplanation.title: Substitution or elimination
simpleExplanation.summary: The C–X bond polarises so C carries partial positive charge. Nucleophiles attack in substitution; hot base can eliminate HX instead.
steps: Hot ethanolic OH⁻ promotes elimination → alkene.
CFCs catalyse ozone destruction in stratosphere — now largely banned.

sections.content: **Halogenoalkanes** combine **mechanistic organic chemistry** with **environmental chemistry**. Examiners test **SN1 vs SN2** with **primary/secondary/tertiary** classification every year.

items: Nucleophile strength: CN⁻ > OH⁻ > NH₃ > H₂O
Leaving group ability: I⁻ > Br⁻ > Cl⁻ > F⁻
SN1: two steps, rate = k[R-X] only
